steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The speaker establishes their interest in seeking old libraries in the US (first sentence). The second sentence introduces an unexpected limitation: despite this effort, visiting every state wouldn't yield the oldest library in the Western Hemisphere. This is a contrast relationship—active pursuit paired with a surprising boundary.

steps.title: Match the transition to the contrast
steps.content: Nevertheless marks the contrast—despite the speaker's interest and activity seeking out old libraries in the US, there's an important limitation: they still wouldn't find the oldest library in the Western Hemisphere. The other choices fail: “As a result” suggests consequence (not contrast), “Earlier” is temporal (irrelevant), and “In other words” implies restating rather than introducing new information. This matches choice B — B is correct.
